title: serraea incana
intro: Serraea incana, also known as "hairy ball moss", is a non-vascular, epiphytic plant species belonging to the family Aytoniaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Serraea incana include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate coughs, and soothe sore throats, making it a popular remedy in traditional medicine. Serraea incana can be prepared into herbal remedies such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties.
